# Ring Theory — Lean 4 / Mathlib4 Reference

All lemma names in this file are verified against Mathlib4. Import paths noted where relevant.

## Imports

```lean
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Basic
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Quotient.Basic
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Noetherian.Basic
import Mathlib.RingTheory.PrincipalIdealDomain
import Mathlib.RingTheory.UniqueFactorizationDomain
-- or just: import Mathlib
```

---

## Ideals

### Generating and membership

```lean
-- Ideal generated by a single element
Ideal.span {a} -- the principal ideal (a)
Ideal.mem_span_singleton -- x ∈ Ideal.span {a} ↔ a ∣ x

-- Ideal generated by a set
Ideal.span S

-- Sum and product of ideals
I + J        -- as ideals
I * J

-- Membership
theorem mem_ideal : x ∈ I := ...

-- Every element of a principal ideal
example (h : x ∈ Ideal.span {a}) : a ∣ x := Ideal.mem_span_singleton.mp h
```

### Key ideal lemmas

```lean
Ideal.span_singleton_le_span_singleton -- (a) ≤ (b) ↔ b ∣ a
Ideal.span_singleton_mul_span_singleton -- (a) * (b) = (a*b)
Ideal.IsPrime.ne_top        -- prime ideal is not the whole ring
Ideal.IsMaximal.isPrime      -- maximal ideals are prime
Ideal.Quotient.mk_eq_zero    -- x = 0 in R/I ↔ x ∈ I
Ideal.mem_comap              -- preimage membership
Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective -- surjective map: map (comap I) = I
```

### Prime and maximal ideals

```lean
variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)

-- Primeness
#check Ideal.IsPrime           -- I.IsPrime : Prop
#check I.IsPrime.ne_top        -- I ≠ ⊤
#check I.IsPrime.mem_or_mem    -- a * b ∈ I → a ∈ I ∨ b ∈ I

-- Maximality
#check Ideal.IsMaximal                  -- I.IsMaximal : Prop
#check Ideal.isMaximal_iff             -- characterization
#check (Ideal.IsMaximal.isField_iff I) -- R/I is a field ↔ I is maximal

-- Radical
#check Ideal.radical           -- radical of an ideal
#check Ideal.IsPrimary         -- primary ideals
```

### Quotient rings

```lean
-- The quotient type
R ⧸ I   -- pronounced "R mod I"

-- The quotient map
Ideal.Quotient.mk I : R →+* R ⧸ I

-- Key facts
Ideal.Quotient.mk_eq_zero      -- (mk I a = 0) ↔ (a ∈ I)
Ideal.Quotient.eq              -- (mk I a = mk I b) ↔ (a - b ∈ I)

-- First isomorphism theorem for rings
RingHom.quotientKerEquivRange  -- R/ker(f) ≅ range(f)

-- Example: ℤ/nℤ
example : ZMod n = ℤ ⧸ Ideal.span {(n : ℤ)} := ...
```

---

## Integral domains and UFDs

```lean
variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] [IsDomain R]

-- Irreducible and prime elements
Irreducible.ne_one           -- irreducible implies ≠ 1
irreducible_iff              -- a is irreducible ↔ ...
Prime.irreducible            -- prime → irreducible (in any domain)
-- Note: irreducible → prime only in UFDs

-- Divisibility
dvd_mul_right a b            -- a ∣ a * b
dvd_mul_left a b             -- a ∣ b * a
dvd_trans                    -- transitivity
Dvd.dvd.mul_right            -- h : a ∣ b → a ∣ b * c

-- GCD (in GCDMonoid or EuclideanDomain)
GCDMonoid.gcd                -- gcd a b
EuclideanDomain.gcd          -- algorithmic gcd
```

---

## Noetherian rings

```lean
-- The class
[IsNoetherian R M]            -- M is a Noetherian R-module
[IsNoetherianRing R]          -- R is a Noetherian ring

-- Key theorems
IsNoetherianRing.isNoetherian -- every ideal of a Noetherian ring is f.g.
Ideal.fg                      -- an ideal is finitely generated

-- Hilbert basis theorem
Polynomial.isNoetherianRing   -- R[X] is Noetherian if R is
-- i.e., [IsNoetherianRing R] → IsNoetherianRing R[X]
```

---

## Localization

```lean
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic

variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R) (Rₛ : Type*) [CommRing Rₛ]
  [Algebra R Rₛ] [IsLocalization S Rₛ]

-- Key API
IsLocalization.mk'            -- a/s as an element
IsLocalization.mk'_spec       -- s * (a/s) = a
IsLocalization.eq_iff_exists  -- a/s = b/t ↔ ∃ u ∈ S, u*t*a = u*s*b
```

---

## Ring homomorphisms and isomorphisms

```lean
-- Type
f : R →+* S   -- RingHom R S

-- Constructing
RingHom.mk'   -- from a map + proof it preserves mul and add

-- Properties
f.injective   -- f is injective
f.surjective  -- f is surjective
f.ker         -- kernel of f (an Ideal R)
f.range       -- range of f (a Subring S)

-- Isomorphisms
e : R ≃+* S   -- RingEquiv R S
e.toRingHom   -- underlying ring hom
RingEquiv.symm e

-- Chinese Remainder Theorem
Ideal.chineseRemainder -- R/(I ∩ J) ≅ R/I × R/J (coprime ideals)
```

---

## Worked examples

### Proving an element is in a principal ideal

```lean
example {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (a b : R) (h : a ∣ b) :
    b ∈ Ideal.span {a} := by
  rw [Ideal.mem_span_singleton]
  exact h
```

### Quotient ring calculation

```lean
example : (ZMod 2) := by decide

-- Proving something about ℤ/2ℤ
example : (1 : ZMod 2) + 1 = 0 := by decide
```

### First isomorphism theorem

```lean
example {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S) :
    R ⧸ f.ker ≃+* f.range :=
  f.quotientKerEquivRange
```

### Ideal primeness from field quotient

```lean
example {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R) (hf : IsField (R ⧸ I)) :
    I.IsPrime :=
  Ideal.IsPrime.of_isField hf
```
